project.name: Sylphx Skills
project.summary: Public static instructions, compact runtime constitution, mandatory AutoSync, and agent-owned Codex, Claude, and Grok enrollment into the canonical Sylphx Enact SaaS.

goals: Own canonical static instructions under skills/<id>/.
Let a user install or update Codex, Claude Code, and Grok Build by giving the repository to an agent.
Converge the complete Skill generation and compact persistent constitution without replacing unrelated user state.
Enroll the canonical https://enact.sylphx.com/api/mcp SaaS resource through validated OAuth metadata and runtime-native configuration.
Publish Sylphx working methods and Platform guidance as useful public product surfaces.
nonGoals: Own live work, organization-wide adoption, effect state, or customer data.
Operate a benchmark laboratory, admission authority, hosted marketplace, or Enact.
Preserve retired instruction copies outside Git history.

owns.name: Static instruction packages
owns.description: Public standards, procedures, profiles, adapters, and self-contained references.

owns.name: Runtime synchronization
owns.description: Small install, update, status, clear, and auto-sync adapters for Codex, Claude Code, and Grok Build.

owns.name: Agent-owned installation
owns.description: A repository-plus-intent contract, compact persistent constitution, exact readback, and fresh-context adoption boundary.

owns.name: Remote integration enrollment
owns.description: Credential-free discovery validation and runtime-native OAuth registration for the canonical Sylphx Enact SaaS resource.

doesNotOwn: Live selected-repository adoption, work claims, effects, or operational customer state.
Product-local decisions, runtime implementation, or model benchmark results.

allowedDependencies.surface: Standard library, native runtime MCP CLIs, and npx bootstrap

forbiddenCouplings: No hard-coded credential, tenant grant, customer-data, benchmark, or model-provider dependency; the canonical Sylphx SaaS hostname is product identity.
No generated or secondary writable copy of Skill semantics.

verificationCommands: isolated runtime fresh-context evaluation when installation changes
delivery.terminalBoundary: Exact default-branch tree passes package and installer integrity, public package inspection, and any installation change has typed clean-runtime evidence for authenticated supported providers.

commercial.metricsSourceOfTruth: Package and product telemetry only where separately authorized.
guardrails: Do not represent MIT-licensed public text as exclusive.
Commercial services may include managed updates, private packages, profiles, integrations, verification, and support.
